title: ConvertKit vs ActiveCampaign (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit vs ActiveCampaign. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool is best for your business.
h1: ConvertKit vs ActiveCampaign: Feature Comparison & Pricing Guide
intro: ConvertKit and ActiveCampaign are both powerful email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit is purpose-built for creators, bloggers, and course sellers with a free tier and visual tools, while ActiveCampaign offers a more comprehensive solution combining email, CRM, and advanced automation for growing businesses.

comparison_table.tool_b: Advanced automation, lead scoring, dynamic content

choose_a_if: You're a creator or course seller needing landing pages and commerce features
You want to start free and scale affordably with up to 1,000 subscribers
You prefer visual automation builders over complex workflows
choose_b_if: You need integrated CRM and advanced lead scoring for sales teams
You require SMS marketing alongside email campaigns
You want the most comprehensive automation and customer tracking features

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ConvertKit is better for creators and bloggers who want a free tier with commerce tools, while ActiveCampaign is superior for businesses needing CRM, advanced automation, and SMS capabilities. ConvertKit has lower Trustpilot ratings (3.2 vs 4.1), suggesting user satisfaction concerns, but remains excellent for its target audience.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: ConvertKit offers a free tier for up to 1,000 subscribers, making it cheaper to start. However, ActiveCampaign begins at just $15/month, undercutting ConvertKit's paid plans which start at $29/month. For small budgets, ConvertKit's free tier wins; for paid plans, ActiveCampaign offers better value.

faq.q: Can I switch from ActiveCampaign to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists and email sequences, though you may need to manually rebuild automations as they use different builders. ConvertKit's visual editor is simpler, so complex ActiveCampaign workflows may need redesigning. Plan for migration time and test thoroughly before switching.
